data_coverage_summary: Samsara Telematics exposes per-vehicle snapshot, history, and feed access for GPS, engine state, odometer, fault-code, fuel, and auxiliary-input readings, making it the primary read surface for fleet location and onboard diagnostic telemetry.
buyer_guidance: Use Telematics when Last Mile needs authoritative location, movement, and vehicle-state reads from Samsara. It is strong for ingest, sync, alerting, and analytics, but the documented surface is read-oriented and does not provide operational writeback into vehicle state through this product area.
overview: The Telematics guide documents three read surfaces over the same vehicle stats model: `GET /fleet/vehicles/stats` for current snapshots, `GET /fleet/vehicles/stats/history` for backfill or time-range queries, and `GET /fleet/vehicles/stats/feed` for cursor-based synchronization. The documented stat types include GPS, engine states, OBD and GPS odometer values, fault codes, fuel levels, engine runtime, and many other diagnostics, which makes this the deepest Samsara product for operational telemetry extraction.

available_data.description: The same fleet telemetry model is exposed as snapshot, historical report, and incremental feed APIs.
data_points: historical stat series for a requested time range
per-vehicle IDs and names alongside returned stat payloads

available_data.description: The documented diagnostic types show the specific readings available through the vehicle stats APIs.
data_points: GPS location, heading, speed, and reverse-geocoded address via `gps`
OBD and GPS odometer readings via `obdOdometerMeters` and `gpsOdometerMeters`
fuel, voltage, RPM, temperature, and auxiliary input readings

available_data.description: The guide documents cursor-based polling and decoration semantics for building analytics or near-real-time integrations.
data_points: up to three primary stat types per query
decorations to join one stat to another at event time

ingest_considerations: Use the feed endpoint for continuous sync; the guide explicitly positions history for backfill and ad hoc retrieval rather than ongoing replication.
Request only the stat types needed for the use case because the endpoints are timeseries-based and type-filtered.
OBD and GPS odometer coverage vary by vehicle, so integrations should request both when mileage matters.
This product area is read-only in the published guide, so any orchestration workflow requiring writeback should use another Samsara domain.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Backed by the Telematics guide sections documenting GET /fleet/vehicles/stats, GET /fleet/vehicles/stats/history, GET /fleet/vehicles/stats/feed, plus the published diagnostic types table for GPS, engine state, odometer, and fault-code reads.
